features.name: Meeting Settings Hub + Transcript Fix + Heatmap Skeleton

stories.title: Create Meeting Settings Hub page with recorder cards
acceptance: New page at /meetings/settings shows only connected recorders as cards/tiles
Each card shows recorder icon, name, and connection status badge
Cards are clickable and navigate to /meetings/settings/:recorder
Back button at top navigates to /meetings
Empty state shown when no recorders connected with link to /integrations
stories.description:
Create a new MeetingSettingsHub page component that:
1. Uses useFathomIntegration(), useFirefliesIntegration(), useNotetakerIntegration() hooks to check isConnected
2. Renders a card/tile for each connected recorder with: icon (Video/Mic lucide icons), name, status badge
3. Cards navigate to /meetings/settings/:recorder on click
4. Has a back button (ArrowLeft icon + 'Back to Meetings') linking to /meetings
5. Shows empty state with message 'No meeting recorders configured' and Button linking to /integrations
6. Add route in MeetingsPage.tsx: <Route path='settings' element={<MeetingSettingsHub />} />
7. Update UnifiedMeetingsList.tsx Settings button (line 643) to navigate to /meetings/settings instead of /settings/meeting-settings

Design: Follow the existing SettingsPageWrapper pattern for consistent look. Cards should use the same styling as IntegrationCard in /integrations.

stories.title: Create individual recorder settings detail page
acceptance: Route /meetings/settings/:recorder renders the correct settings tab component
Supports recorder values: notetaker, fathom, fireflies
Has back button navigating to /meetings/settings hub
Invalid recorder param shows 404 or redirects to hub
Existing NotetakerSettingsTab, FathomSettingsTab, FirefliesSettingsTab components render correctly within new wrapper
stories.description:
Create MeetingRecorderSettingsPage that:
1. Reads :recorder param from URL
2. Maps 'notetaker' -> NotetakerSettingsTab, 'fathom' -> FathomSettingsTab, 'fireflies' -> FirefliesSettingsTab
3. Wraps selected component in a layout with:
   - Back button (ArrowLeft + 'Back to Meeting Settings') linking to /meetings/settings
   - Page title based on recorder name (e.g. '60 Notetaker Settings')
   - The settings tab component
4. If recorder param is invalid, navigate to /meetings/settings
5. Add route in MeetingsPage.tsx: <Route path='settings/:recorder' element={<MeetingRecorderSettingsPage />} />

IMPORTANT: Route order matters - 'settings/:recorder' must come after 'settings' index route.

stories.title: Standardize transcript validation across edge functions
acceptance: New shared helper validateTranscript() in _shared/transcriptValidation.ts
All 5 edge functions use the shared helper instead of inline char-length checks
Minimum threshold is 20 words (not 50/100 chars) for user-initiated analysis
Error messages distinguish 'no transcript' from 'transcript too short'
Consistent 422 status code across all functions

stories.title: Fix Activity Heatmap skeleton to match actual component
acceptance: Dashboard.tsx heatmap skeleton matches actual Heatmap component grid structure
Cell rounding uses rounded-lg (matching ActivityHeatmapCell)
Week label skeleton matches flex items-center justify-end pattern (not h-8 fixed)
Header row includes empty first cell for grid alignment
Skeleton legend row matches actual legend structure
stories.description:
Fix both skeleton locations to match actual Heatmap component: 1. Dashboard.tsx (lines 950-972) — Suspense fallback for heatmap tab: Current problems: - Missing empty first cell in header row (line 955 has
but week row skeletons use h-8 w-full) - Week label skeleton: `h-8 w-full rounded` → should be `h-full w-5 rounded justify-self-end` (matches actual text-xs label) - Cell rounding: `rounded` → `rounded-lg` (matching ActivityHeatmapCell line 37) - 5 hardcoded weeks is fine for a skeleton - Missing legend row at bottom Fix to: ```tsx
{['Mon','Tue','Wed','Thu','Fri','Sat','Sun'].map((d) => ( ))} {Array.from({ length: 5 }).map((_, w) => ( {Array.from({ length: 7 }).map((_, d) => ( ))} ))}
{[0,1,2,3,4,5,6].map(i => )}
``` 2. Heatmap.tsx HeatmapSkeleton() (lines 14-80): - Line 61: Cell rounding `rounded` → `rounded-lg` to match ActivityHeatmapCell - Line 58: Week label `h-full w-5 rounded justify-self-end` is correct, keep as-is - Structure is already well-matched, just the cell rounding fix needed

stories.title: Add route redirects and clean up old meeting settings paths
acceptance: /settings/meeting-settings redirects to /meetings/settings
/settings/integrations/fathom redirects to /meetings/settings/fathom
/settings/integrations/fireflies redirects to /meetings/settings/fireflies
/settings/integrations/60-notetaker redirects to /meetings/settings/notetaker
/meetings/recordings/settings redirects to /meetings/settings/notetaker
stories.description:
Update App.tsx routes:

1. Change existing redirects (lines 655-660) to point to new locations:
   - `/settings/meeting-settings` → `<Navigate to='/meetings/settings' replace />`
   - `/settings/integrations/fathom` → `<Navigate to='/meetings/settings/fathom' replace />`
   - `/settings/integrations/fireflies` → `<Navigate to='/meetings/settings/fireflies' replace />`
   - `/settings/integrations/60-notetaker` → `<Navigate to='/meetings/settings/notetaker' replace />`
   - `/meetings/recordings/settings` → `<Navigate to='/meetings/settings/notetaker' replace />`

2. Remove the standalone MeetingSettingsPage route (line 655) since it's now inside MeetingsPage routing

3. Keep the MeetingSettingsPage import removal clean — verify no other references exist

Note: The /meetings/* wildcard route already handles /meetings/settings and /meetings/settings/:recorder via MeetingsPage sub-routes.

parallelGroups.reason: No file overlap — frontend hub, backend edge functions, and heatmap skeleton are independent
